what_conformance_missed: LD12 proof rollup (debug_report._snyk_rollup) trusted the prose-supplied newFindings instead of recomputing from before/after counts; a real Snyk regression (before=0,after=5,newFindings=0) rendered as clean. PART A PASS on internally-consistent seeded fixtures.

LD12 deviation table (debug_report.build_deviation_table) set applied:true on drift PASS alone, not gated on the finding's route; under a finding_id collision a research finding rendered as a proven applied fix. PART A PASS on the happy-path fixtures.

Tier-2 apply park overwrote the Tier-1 escalate artifact (.kata/escalations/<task-id>.json) for the same task on the stateful-destroy-apply path; one of two human-required reasons could be silently lost. Per-build D98 (D119) passed it; the D117-D121 cross-cut ad-val caught it.
guard_ref: kata-orchestrate Tier-1-escalate-parks-and-returns + escalation.py write_escalation non-clobber guard

what_conformance_missed: debug_report _snyk_rollup did not floor effective_new at 0; a negative newFindings artifact could deflate the advisory regression total (could NOT flip clean=True). Per-build D98 (D117) passed it; the cross-cut ad-val caught it.

exec-safety registry completeness was human-enforced only (no test asserted every subprocess sink is registered); a shell=False sink could slip through unregistered. The mis-described kata_install row (D121) was a live instance. Cross-cut ad-val caught it.

Benchmark dual-gate runner (benchmark.run_dual_gate) was never wired into kata-orchestrate; score_arms ran with no gate data -> f2p_total==0 -> Q=1.0 for any floor-passing arm. Quality axis non-functional. PART A (kata-evaluate) PASSED (tested score_arms in isolation with injected booleans); D98 caught the dead wiring.

benchmark.run_dual_gate joined control-supplied (external-trust) test-IDs with no ..-guard -> a ../.. node-ID escaped the clone root and pytest would import/exec it; the new external field feeding mutation_check.run_named_test was unregistered in exec-safety.md. PART A passed; D98 caught.

kata-benchmark-report cited scorecard fields the engine never emitted (q_score/floor_pass/f2p_green/honesty-block/recommendations) + a reversed emit_scorecard arg order; the 'engine-pinned honesty' claim was prose-only. PART A passed; D98 caught.
guard_ref: benchmark.py real honesty/recommendations blocks + skill cites-only-real-fields

what_conformance_missed: Benchmark definition+criteria+delta chain was BUILT+tested but never WIRED into the orchestrate flow: def_out required-but-never-written; criteria_ref produced-never-consumed (no load_criteria call); repeat_from delta sameDefinition structurally always False (fresh benchmark_id per run). PART A + D98 passed (unit/injected-data); the deep end-to-end ad-val caught the unwired chain.
guard_ref: D124 wiring: content_hash->build_def->write_def(def_out); load_criteria->run_dual_gate; repeat_from reuses prior benchmark_id->sameDefinition

what_conformance_missed: The dual-gate ran a control's tests with NO cwd/import-context -> pytest ran in the harness venv, clone not on sys.path -> any real importing control (from src import X) failed collection -> Q=0 despite floor PASS. Quality axis non-functional for real controls. PART A + D98 passed (self-contained/injected booleans); deep ad-val caught it with a realistic importing fixture.
guard_ref: D124 mutation_check.run_named_test cwd param + run_dual_gate cwd=clone_root + PYTHONPATH

what_conformance_missed: Composite metric read raw .kata/usage.json on the scorer path; a negative/NaN costUSD won rank-1 + the metric was non-deterministic, and omitting your worst Axis-C dimension won (exclude-from-mean over-fix). The D98 non-negativity guard was on the WRONG side of the trust boundary (build_usage writer, not the score_arms reader). Ranking inversion. PART A + D98 passed; deep ad-val caught it.
guard_ref: D124 _validate_numeric (isfinite&>=0) on read path + worst-case imputation + build_usage isfinite
